pages.summary: Returns generic pointer obtained by executing PTX cvta.shared instruction on rawbits value.

pages.title: 10.15.3 __isConstant()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in constant memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.1 __isGlobal()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in global memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.4 __isGridConstant()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of kernel parameter annotated with __grid_constant__, otherwise 0. Only supported for compute architectures >= 7.x. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.5 __isLocal()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in local memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.2 __isShared()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in shared memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.14.1.11 __nv_atomic_compare_exchange() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Generic atomic compare-and-exchange function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Compares value at ptr with value at expected. If equal, returns true and stores desired to ptr. Otherwise, returns false and stores ptr value to expected. Ignores weak parameter, uses stronger of success/failure orders. Supports 2, 4, 8, or 16-byte types. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for 16-byte types, sm_70+ for 2-byte types, and sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.12 __nv_atomic_compare_exchange_n() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Non-generic atomic compare-and-exchange function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Compares value at ptr with value at expected. If equal, returns true and stores desired to ptr. Otherwise, returns false and stores ptr value to expected. Ignores weak parameter, uses stronger of success/failure orders. Supports integral types of 2, 4, 8, or 16 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for 16-byte types, sm_70+ for 2-byte types, and sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.9 __nv_atomic_exchange() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Generic atomic exchange function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, stores to ret. Reads value at val, stores to ptr. Supports 4, 8, or 16-byte types. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for 16-byte types and cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.10 __nv_atomic_exchange_n() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Non-generic atomic exchange function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, returns it. Stores val to ptr. Supports integral types of 4, 8, or 16 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for 16-byte types and cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.13 __nv_atomic_fetch_add() and __nv_atomic_add() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic addition functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, adds val, stores result back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_add returns old value; __nv_atomic_add returns void. Supports unsigned int, int, unsigned long long, float, or double. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.2.6 __nv_atomic_fetch_and() and __nv_atomic_and() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic bitwise AND functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, ANDs with val, stores result back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_and returns old value; __nv_atomic_and returns void. Supports integral types of 4 or 8 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.16 __nv_atomic_fetch_max() and __nv_atomic_max() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic maximum functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, compares with val, stores larger value back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_max returns old value; __nv_atomic_max returns void. Supports unsigned int, int, unsigned long long, or long long. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.15 __nv_atomic_fetch_min() and __nv_atomic_min() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic minimum functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, compares with val, stores smaller value back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_min returns old value; __nv_atomic_min returns void. Supports unsigned int, int, unsigned long long, or long long. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.2.4 __nv_atomic_fetch_or() and __nv_atomic_or() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic bitwise OR functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, ORs with val, stores result back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_or returns old value; __nv_atomic_or returns void. Supports integral types of 4 or 8 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.1.14 __nv_atomic_fetch_sub() and __nv_atomic_sub() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic subtraction functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, subtracts val, stores result back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_sub returns old value; __nv_atomic_sub returns void. Supports unsigned int, int, unsigned long long, float, or double. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.2.5 __nv_atomic_fetch_xor() and __nv_atomic_xor() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic bitwise XOR functions introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at ptr, XORs with val, stores result back to ptr. __nv_atomic_fetch_xor returns old value; __nv_atomic_xor returns void. Supports integral types of 4 or 8 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.14.3.1 __nv_atomic_load() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Generic atomic load function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Loads value at ptr and writes to ret. Supports 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16-byte types. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_70+ for 16-byte types, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals. order cannot be __NV_ATOMIC_RELEASE or __NV_ATOMIC_ACQ_REL.

pages.title: 10.14.3.2 __nv_atomic_load_n() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Non-generic atomic load function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Loads value at ptr and returns it. Supports integral types of 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_70+ for 16-byte types, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals. order cannot be __NV_ATOMIC_RELEASE or __NV_ATOMIC_ACQ_REL.

pages.title: 10.14.3.3 __nv_atomic_store() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Generic atomic store function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Reads value at val and stores to ptr. Supports 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16-byte types. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_70+ for 16-byte types, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals. order cannot be __NV_ATOMIC_CONSUME, __NV_ATOMIC_ACQUIRE, or __NV_ATOMIC_ACQ_REL.

pages.title: 10.14.3.4 __nv_atomic_store_n() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Non-generic atomic store function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Stores val to ptr. Supports integral types of 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16 bytes. Requires sm_60+ for memory order/scope, sm_70+ for 16-byte types, sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals. order cannot be __NV_ATOMIC_CONSUME, __NV_ATOMIC_ACQUIRE, or __NV_ATOMIC_ACQ_REL.

pages.title: 10.14.3.5 __nv_atomic_thread_fence() (CUDA 12.8)
pages.summary: Atomic thread fence function introduced in CUDA 12.8. Establishes ordering between memory accesses based on specified memory order. Thread scope parameter specifies observing threads. Requires sm_90+ for cluster scope. Arguments order and scope must be integer literals.

pages.title: 10.17 Alloca Function
pages.summary: Allocates size bytes of memory in the stack frame of the caller. Returns 16-byte aligned pointer in device code. Automatically freed on function return. Requires compute capability 5.2+. On Windows, <malloc.h> must be included. May cause stack overflow.

pages.title: 10.18.3 __assume()
pages.summary: Compiler optimization hint. Allows compiler to assume Boolean argument is true. Undefined behavior if false at runtime. Unspecified behavior if argument has side effects.

pages.title: 10.14.2.1 atomicAnd()
pages.summary: Atomic bitwise AND function. Reads 32-bit or 64-bit word at address, computes (old & val), stores result back. Returns old value. 64-bit version requires compute capability 5.0 or higher.

pages.title: 10.14.2.2 atomicOr()
pages.summary: Atomic bitwise OR function. Reads 32-bit or 64-bit word at address, computes (old | val), stores result back. Returns old value. 64-bit version requires compute capability 5.0 or higher.

pages.title: 10.14.2.3 atomicXor()
pages.summary: Atomic bitwise XOR function. Reads 32-bit or 64-bit word at address, computes (old ^ val), stores result back. Returns old value. 64-bit version requires compute capability 5.0 or higher.

pages.title: Built-in Vector Types
pages.summary: Describes built-in vector types (char, short, int, long, float, double, dim3) derived from basic types, their component accessors (x, y, z, w), constructors, and alignment requirements.

pages.title: 10.18.2 __builtin_assume()
pages.summary: Compiler optimization hint. Allows compiler to assume Boolean argument is true. Undefined behavior if false at runtime. Unspecified behavior if argument has side effects.

pages.title: 10.18.1 __builtin_assume_aligned()
pages.summary: Compiler optimization hint. Allows compiler to assume pointer is aligned to at least align bytes. Returns argument pointer. Supports two-parameter and three-parameter versions (with offset).

pages.title: 10.18.4 __builtin_expect()
pages.summary: Compiler optimization hint. Indicates expected value of expression for branch prediction. Returns value of exp.

pages.title: 10.18.5 __builtin_unreachable()
pages.summary: Compiler optimization hint. Indicates control flow never reaches this point. Undefined behavior if reached at runtime.

pages.title: Conditional Handles
pages.summary: Covers `cudaGraphConditionalHandle` creation and lifecycle. Handles are tied to a single node, cannot be destroyed, and can be initialized with a default value using `cudaGraphCondAssignDefault`. Defaults update during whole-graph updates.

pages.title: Conditional IF Nodes
pages.summary: Covers IF conditional nodes that execute a body graph once if the condition is non-zero. Supports an optional second body graph (else branch) executed if zero (added in CUDA 12.8). Includes creation and population examples.

pages.title: Conditional WHILE Nodes
pages.summary: Covers WHILE conditional nodes that execute their body graph repeatedly as long as the condition remains non-zero. Condition is evaluated at execution and after each body completion. Includes creation and loop example.

pages.title: Guidelines for cuGetProcAddress
pages.summary: Provides guidelines for using cuGetProcAddress, including version matching and driver version checks, and explains how to determine failure reasons using CUdriverProcAddressQueryResult.

pages.title: Guidelines for cuGetProcAddress
pages.summary: Provides guidelines for using cuGetProcAddress, including version matching and driver version checks, and explains how to determine failure reasons using CUdriverProcAddressQueryResult.

pages.title: atomicAdd()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic addition operation on 16-bit, 32-bit, 64-bit, and vector types (float2, float4, __half, __nv_bfloat16) in global or shared memory. Returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicCAS()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic compare-and-swap operation on 16-bit, 32-bit, 64-bit, and 128-bit types in global or shared memory. Returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicDec()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic decrement operation on a 32-bit unsigned integer in global or shared memory. Returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicExch()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic exchange operation on 16-bit, 32-bit, 64-bit, and 128-bit types in global or shared memory. Stores a new value and returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicInc()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic increment operation on a 32-bit unsigned integer in global or shared memory. Returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicMax()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic maximum operation on 32-bit and 64-bit signed/unsigned integers in global or shared memory. Returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicMin()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic minimum operation on 32-bit and 64-bit signed/unsigned integers in global or shared memory. Returns the old value.

pages.title: atomicSub()
pages.summary: Performs an atomic subtraction operation on 32-bit integers in global or shared memory. Computes (old - val) and returns the old value.

pages.title: Compute Capability 10.0
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 10.0 (Blackwell) including fifth-gen Tensor Cores, 256KB unified cache/shared memory, and extended MMA acceleration features.

pages.title: Compute Capability 12.0
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 12.0 (Blackwell variant) including fifth-gen Tensor Cores, 100KB unified cache/shared memory, and specialized MMA acceleration features.

pages.title: Compute Capability 5.x
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 5.x including SM components (CUDA cores, SFUs, schedulers), cache hierarchy (L1/texture, L2), global memory caching via __ldg(), and shared memory bank organization.

pages.title: Compute Capability 6.x
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 6.x (Pascal) including updated core counts, scheduler configurations, cache sizes, and memory behavior inheriting from 5.x.

pages.title: Compute Capability 7.x
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 7.x (Volta/Turing) including Tensor Cores, unified data cache/shared memory partitioning, independent thread scheduling changes, __syncwarp() usage, and dynamic shared memory carveout via cudaFuncSetAttribute().

pages.title: Compute Capability 8.x
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 8.x (Ampere) including third/fourth-gen Tensor Cores, unified cache/shared memory sizes (192KB/128KB), and shared memory carveout configurations.

pages.title: Compute Capability 9.0
pages.summary: Architecture specifications for Compute Capability 9.0 (Hopper) including fourth-gen Tensor Cores with FP8 support, 256KB unified cache/shared memory, and specialized MMA acceleration features.

pages.title: CUDA Environment Variables
pages.summary: Lists and describes various CUDA environment variables such as CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, CUDA_MANAGED_FORCE_DEVICE, CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER, CUDA_CACHE_DISABLE, CUDA_FORCE_PTX_JIT, CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING, and CUDA_LOG_FILE.

pages.title: cudaGraphAddMemAllocNode
pages.summary: API for adding memory allocation nodes to graphs with explicit peer access descriptors and location properties.

pages.title: cudaGraphInstantiateFlagAutoFreeOnLaunch
pages.summary: Flag allowing graph relaunch with unfreed allocations by automatically inserting asynchronous frees.

pages.title: cudaGraphUpload
pages.summary: API to separate allocation cost from launch by performing mappings immediately and associating with an upload stream.

pages.title: CUDA Environment Variables
pages.summary: Lists and describes various CUDA environment variables such as CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, CUDA_MANAGED_FORCE_DEVICE, CUDA_DEVICE_ORDER, CUDA_CACHE_DISABLE, CUDA_FORCE_PTX_JIT, CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING, and CUDA_LOG_FILE.

pages.summary: Returns generic pointer obtained by executing PTX cvta.const instruction on rawbits value.

pages.title: 10.16.3 __cvta_generic_to_constant()
pages.summary: Returns result of executing PTX cvta.to.const instruction on generic address denoted by ptr.

pages.title: 10.16.1 __cvta_generic_to_global()
pages.summary: Returns result of executing PTX cvta.to.global instruction on generic address denoted by ptr.

pages.summary: Returns result of executing PTX cvta.to.local instruction on generic address denoted by ptr.

pages.summary: Returns result of executing PTX cvta.to.shared instruction on generic address denoted by ptr.

pages.summary: Returns generic pointer obtained by executing PTX cvta.global instruction on rawbits value.

pages.summary: Returns generic pointer obtained by executing PTX cvta.local instruction on rawbits value.

pages.title: 10.16.6 __cvta_shared_to_generic()
pages.summary: Returns generic pointer obtained by executing PTX cvta.shared instruction on rawbits value.

pages.title: Device Graph Creation
pages.summary: Covers the explicit instantiation requirement for device graphs using the `cudaGraphInstantiateFlagDeviceLaunch` flag. Structure is fixed at instantiation, and instantiation must occur on the host.

pages.title: Device Graph Update
pages.summary: Covers that device graphs can only be updated from the host and require re-upload to the device for changes to take effect. Launching from the device during an update causes undefined behavior.

pages.title: Device Graph Upload
pages.summary: Covers methods to upload device graphs to the GPU to populate device resources: explicit upload via `cudaGraphUpload()`, upload during instantiation via `cudaGraphInstantiateWithParams()`, or implicit upload via an initial host launch.

pages.title: Device Launch Execution
pages.summary: Covers launching device graphs from host or device using `cudaGraphLaunch()`. Notes that device-side launch is per-thread, requiring users to select a single thread for a given graph to avoid conflicts.

pages.title: DPX (Dynamic Programming eXtensions)
pages.summary: DPX is a set of hardware-accelerated functions (compute capability 9+) for min/max, fused add/min/max, and optional ReLU operations on 16/32-bit signed/unsigned integers. Widely used in dynamic programming algorithms like Smith-Waterman, Needleman-Wunsch, and Floyd-Warshall.

pages.title: Error Log Management
pages.summary: Describes the Error Log Management mechanism for reporting CUDA API errors in plain-English format, including activation via CUDA_LOG_FILE, log output format, and APIs like cuLogsRegisterCallback and cuLogsDumpToMemory.

pages.title: Error Log Management
pages.summary: Describes the Error Log Management mechanism for reporting CUDA API errors in plain-English format, including activation via CUDA_LOG_FILE, log output format, and APIs like cuLogsRegisterCallback and cuLogsDumpToMemory.

pages.title: Fire and Forget Launch
pages.summary: Covers the fire-and-forget mode where the launched graph runs independently as a child of the parent graph's execution environment. Limits up to 120 fire-and-forget graphs per parent execution, resetting between launches.

pages.title: Function Execution Space Specifiers
pages.summary: Defines __global__, __device__, __host__, __noinline__, __forceinline__, and __inline_hint__ specifiers, their usage rules, cross-execution space call restrictions, and undefined behavior conditions.

pages.title: Individual Node Enable
pages.summary: Covers enabling or disabling specific kernel, memset, and memcpy nodes within an instantiated graph using `cudaGraphNodeSetEnabled()`. Disabled nodes act as empty nodes until re-enabled, and parameter updates while disabled apply upon re-enablement.

pages.title: Individual Node Update
pages.summary: Covers updating parameters of instantiated graph nodes directly using specific setter APIs to avoid instantiation overhead. Applicable when the number of nodes to update is small relative to the total graph size.

pages.title: 10.15.3 __isConstant()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in constant memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.1 __isGlobal()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in global memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.4 __isGridConstant()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of kernel parameter annotated with __grid_constant__, otherwise 0. Only supported for compute architectures >= 7.x. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.5 __isLocal()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in local memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: 10.15.2 __isShared()
pages.summary: Address space predicate function. Returns 1 if ptr contains generic address of object in shared memory space, otherwise 0. Unspecified behavior if ptr is null.

pages.title: Read-Only Data Cache Load Function (__ldg)
pages.summary: Documentation for the __ldg() intrinsic, which loads data from global memory into the read-only data cache, supporting various primitive and vector types, and requiring compute capability 5.0 or higher.

pages.title: Memory Fence Functions
pages.summary: Explains __threadfence_block(), __threadfence(), and __threadfence_system() for enforcing sequentially-consistent memory ordering across different scopes (block, device, system) to prevent data races.

pages.title: Sibling Launch
pages.summary: Covers sibling launch mode, where the graph is launched as a child of the parent environment rather than the launching graph's environment. Does not gate tail launches enqueued by the launching graph.

pages.title: Tail Launch
pages.summary: Covers the tail launch mode for serial work dependencies on the device. Executes when the parent graph's environment completes. Supports multiple enqueued graphs, strict ordering, and up to 255 pending tail launches per graph.

pages.title: Tail Self-Launch
pages.summary: Covers the ability of a device graph to enqueue itself for a tail launch using `cudaGetCurrentGraphExec()`. Allows device-side looping/recursion. Only one self-launch can be enqueued at a time.

pages.title: Multi-dimensional TMA PTX Wrappers
pages.summary: Lists the CUDA C++ wrapper functions for `cp.async.bulk.tensor` PTX instructions, supporting 1D to 5D bulk tensor asynchronous copies between global and shared memory.

pages.title: Unified Memory Programming
pages.summary: Introduces CUDA Unified Memory, its system requirements, programming model, allocation APIs (cudaMallocManaged, __managed__), and examples demonstrating memory allocation and usage across host and device.

pages.title: Unified Memory Programming
pages.summary: Introduces CUDA Unified Memory, its system requirements, programming model, allocation APIs (cudaMallocManaged, __managed__), and examples demonstrating memory allocation and usage across host and device.

pages.title: Variable Memory Space Specifiers
pages.summary: Covers __device__, __constant__, __shared__, __grid_constant__, __managed__, and __restrict__ specifiers. Details their lifetime, scope, alignment, aliasing implications, and compiler optimization effects.

pages.title: Formatted Output
pages.summary: Formatted output is supported on compute capability 2.x and higher via the in-kernel printf() function. It behaves similarly to the standard C library printf() but outputs to a host-side stream. Each thread executes printf independently. The function returns the number of arguments parsed. Limitations include host-dependent formatting, a 32-argument limit, potential output corruption on win64 with %ld, and a fixed-size circular buffer that flushes on kernel launch, synchronization, memory copies, module loading, context destruction, or stream callbacks. Thread execution order may be affected.

pages.title: Profiler Counter Function
pages.summary: Each multiprocessor has sixteen hardware counters that applications can increment via the __prof_trigger() function. Counters 0-7 are available for applications, while 8-15 are reserved. Values can be retrieved using nvprof --events prof_trigger_0x. Counters reset before each kernel launch.

pages.title: Trap Function
pages.summary: The __trap() function can be called from any device thread to initiate a trap operation. Execution of the kernel is aborted and an interrupt is raised in the host program.
